mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction from price shocks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with > 55% accuracy, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 outside recovery phases, or 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02, or positive return frequency during payroll growth periods drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 900 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of cases when HOUST falls below 900, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation indicate a restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance with p-value above 0.10, or positive return frequency exceeds 55% during concurrent rate hikes and inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects peak economic momentum that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when tested outside late-cycle phases, or 60-day returns are negative in > 55% of cases.
